title: EF-4 tornadoes kill at least 15 across Oklahoma and Kansas, FEMA declares emergency
summary: Deadly EF-4 tornadoes swept through Oklahoma and Kansas, killing at least 15 people and injuring over 200. Multiple towns sustained severe damage. FEMA declared an emergency and deployed response teams.
contents:
# EF-4 Tornadoes Devastate Oklahoma and Kansas Communities

## Impact Assessment
Multiple EF-4 tornadoes swept through Oklahoma and Kansas on May 1, 2026, leaving at least 15 people dead and more than 200 injured. The storms leveled portions of several towns across both states.

## Emergency Response
- FEMA declared a federal emergency for affected areas
- Search and rescue teams deployed to damaged communities
- National Guard activated to support emergency operations
- Red Cross established temporary shelters

## Damage Assessment
The tornadoes, rated EF-4 on the Enhanced Fujita Scale, generated winds exceeding 166 mph. Preliminary reports indicate widespread structural damage to residential areas, commercial buildings, and infrastructure. Power outages affect thousands of customers across the impact zone.

## Detection Timeline
Dataminr's AI-powered detection platform flagged the event minutes before traditional media coverage, providing early warning to emergency management agencies. National Weather Service had issued tornado warnings for the affected regions.

## Tags
